meaning: n.从事破坏活动者,怠工者
examples: So there wasn't a saboteur at all. You made that up?
For a discussion of saboteur data, see my last installment.
Now I feel like a traitor, a saboteur to hundreds of years of progress.
Hunt saboteur Michael Haines, however, said he has seen the law being broken "every single week".
The Saboteur is a nagging, negative mental manifestation that tries to sabotage your efforts to grow and disrupt your desire to achieve new things.
